name: SL Exam Phase 7 - DOCX, Pillow, and plots
description: Single-choice Python exam practice questions generated from Lecture 11: Processing Pictures and Documents.

topics.name: DOCX prerequisites and structure
keypoints: DOCX is XML-based and contains paragraphs and runs

topics.name: Headings, styles, pictures, breaks, tables
keypoints: add_heading returns a paragraph and supports levels 0-9
Run attributes include bold, italic, underline, all_caps

topics.name: Pillow basics
keypoints: RGBA colors use alpha: 0 transparent, 255 opaque
Image.open opens files; Image.new creates images; save writes them

topics.name: Pillow image operations
keypoints: Image properties include width, height, filename, format, size
resize returns new image and expects a tuple
copy and paste work with image objects

topics.name: Pillow drawing and text
keypoints: ImageDraw.Draw(img) creates a drawing object and modifies original image
point, line, rectangle, ellipse draw shapes

topics.name: matplotlib plots
keypoints: Format strings select colors and markers
axis sets viewport; savefig writes file

questions.question: Which command installs the module used in the lecture to create and read DOCX files?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses the python-docx package.

questions.question: Which import is used in the lecture to access the Document class directly?
questions.explanation: The example reads and writes DOCX files with from docx import Document.

questions.question: DOCX files are internally structured using XML rather than being plain text files.
questions.explanation: DOCX is a structured document format and internally stores data using XML.

questions.question: A DOCX document contains paragraphs, and each paragraph can contain one or more Run objects.
questions.explanation: The lecture describes documents as paragraphs, and paragraphs as runs.

questions.question: What is a Run object in python-docx?

options.A: A sequence of characters using one style

questions.explanation: A run is text with one style inside a paragraph.

questions.question: Which structure is closest to the lecture model of DOCX content?
questions.explanation: DOCX text is modeled as paragraphs containing runs.

questions.question: Given doc = Document("mydoc.docx"), what does len(doc.paragraphs) return?

options.A: The number of paragraph objects in the document
options.B: The number of pages in the document
options.C: The number of styles in the document
options.D: The number of words in the first paragraph

questions.explanation: doc.paragraphs is the list of paragraph objects.

questions.question: Which expression reads the plain text of the first paragraph?
questions.explanation: The paragraph text is accessed through the .text attribute.

questions.question: Which expression reads the text of the first run in the second paragraph?
questions.explanation: Runs are inside paragraphs, so the second paragraph is index 1 and first run is index 0.

questions.question: In Python indexing, doc.paragraphs[1] refers to the first paragraph.
questions.explanation: Index 1 refers to the second paragraph. The first paragraph is index 0.

questions.question: Which code creates a new empty DOCX document object?
questions.explanation: Document() creates a new document.

questions.question: Which method saves a python-docx document to a file?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses doc.save(filename).

questions.question: Which call adds a paragraph with the Title style?
questions.explanation: add_paragraph can receive a style argument.

questions.question: doc.add_paragraph("The second paragraph") adds a paragraph with the default style.
questions.explanation: Without a style argument, the paragraph uses the default style.

questions.question: Which method adds a heading to a python-docx document?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses add_heading(text, level=...).

questions.question: A heading in python-docx is a special kind of paragraph.
questions.explanation: The lecture states that a heading is a different kind of paragraph.

questions.question: In python-docx, heading levels go from 0 up to 9.
questions.explanation: The lecture states heading levels are from 0 up to 9.

questions.question: add_heading() returns a paragraph object.
questions.explanation: The lecture explicitly states that add_heading returns a paragraph.

questions.question: Which type of style applies to runs?
questions.explanation: Character styles apply to runs.

questions.question: Which type of style applies to paragraphs?
questions.explanation: Paragraph styles apply to paragraphs.

questions.question: A linked style can apply to both paragraphs and runs.
questions.explanation: The lecture lists linked style as applying to both.

questions.question: When using a paragraph style name as a run character style in Word naming convention, what suffix is added?
questions.explanation: The lecture says to add “ Char”, for example “Caption Char”.

questions.question: Which expression sets the first paragraph style to Title?
questions.explanation: The style attribute of the paragraph is assigned directly.

questions.question: Which expression makes the first run of the second paragraph bold?
questions.explanation: Run formatting attributes such as bold can be assigned directly.

questions.question: Run objects can have attributes such as bold, italic, underline, and all_caps.
questions.explanation: These are listed as run attributes in the lecture.

questions.question: Which method adds a picture to a DOCX document?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses add_picture with optional width or height.

questions.question: Which object is used in the example to express picture width in centimeters?
questions.explanation: The example uses docx.shared.Cm(8).

questions.question: Which method inserts a break inside an existing run?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses runs[i].add_break().

questions.question: Which constant is used in the lecture for a page break?
questions.explanation: The page-break example uses docx.enum.text.WD_BREAK.PAGE.

questions.question: Which method creates a Word table in python-docx?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses add_table(rows=1, cols=2).

questions.question: In the table example, what does table.rows[0].cells access?

options.A: The cells of the first row

questions.explanation: rows[0] is the first row and .cells gives its cells.

questions.question: Which method adds a new row to a DOCX table?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses table.add_row().cells.

questions.question: When assigning numeric data to a DOCX table cell text, the example converts the value with str().
questions.explanation: The example uses row_cells[1].text = str(level).

questions.question: Which command installs the image manipulation package used in the lecture?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses Pillow for image manipulation.

questions.question: Although the package is called Pillow, which module name is used for compatibility?
questions.explanation: The lecture notes that the module still uses the name PIL.

questions.question: Pillow is a fork of the original PIL library.
questions.explanation: The lecture explicitly says Pillow is the fork of the original PIL.

questions.question: What does the A in RGBA stand for?
questions.explanation: Alpha controls transparency/opacity.

questions.question: Which RGBA value represents fully opaque red?
questions.explanation: Red is 255 in the red channel, 0 in green/blue, and 255 alpha is opaque.

questions.question: What alpha value means fully transparent in RGBA?
questions.explanation: Alpha 0 means fully transparent.

questions.question: What alpha value means fully opaque in RGBA?
questions.explanation: Alpha 255 means fully opaque.

questions.question: Which import is used to convert color names into RGB/RGBA values?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses ImageColor from PIL.

questions.question: Which expression returns the RGB tuple for the color name red?
questions.explanation: The example uses ImageColor.getrgb("red").

questions.question: Which expression can return an RGBA value for red?
questions.explanation: The example uses getcolor with mode RGBA.

questions.question: Which function opens an image file with Pillow?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses Image.open(filename).

questions.question: Which method saves an image object to a file?
questions.explanation: Image objects have a save method.

questions.question: PIL recognizes the output file format from the file extension when saving.
questions.explanation: The lecture states that PIL recognizes the extension of the file.

questions.question: Which code creates a new 200x100 red image in memory?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses Image.new(mode, size, color).

questions.question: What type must be used for the size argument in Image.new("RGBA", (200,100), "red")?

options.D: A dictionary with x and y

questions.explanation: The size is passed as a tuple.

questions.question: Which property gives the width of a Pillow image?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists width as an image property.

questions.question: Which property gives the height of a Pillow image?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists height as an image property.

questions.question: What does img.size return?
questions.explanation: The lecture says size returns a tuple (width, height).

questions.question: An image opened from a file can have a filename property.
questions.explanation: The lecture lists filename if opened from the file.

questions.question: The format property is one of the properties listed for a picture object.
questions.explanation: The lecture lists format as a picture property.

questions.question: In Pillow image coordinates, where is point (0, 0)?
questions.explanation: The coordinate diagram shows (0,0) at the top-left corner.

questions.question: What does img.crop((100,100,400,200)) use as its argument?

options.A: A box tuple with left, upper, right, lower coordinates

questions.explanation: Pillow crop uses a rectangular box tuple.

questions.question: In img.crop((100,100,400,200)), what is the cropped width?
questions.explanation: The width is right - left = 400 - 100 = 300.

questions.question: In img.crop((100,100,400,200)), what is the cropped height?
questions.explanation: The height is lower - upper = 200 - 100 = 100.

questions.question: Which call mirrors an image left-to-right?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses transpose with Image.Transpose.FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT.

questions.question: Which call rotates an image by 90 degrees?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses img.rotate(90).

questions.question: What does expand=True do when rotating an image?

options.A: Expands the output image so the rotated image fits

questions.explanation: The lecture says expand=True makes the rotated image fit.

questions.question: Rotating with expand=False can cut corners.
questions.explanation: The lecture says rotated images may have cut corners when expand=False.

questions.question: Which method changes the pixel dimensions of a Pillow image and returns a new image?
questions.explanation: resize returns a new image.

questions.question: resize() modifies the original picture object in place.
questions.explanation: The lecture states resize returns a new image and does not modify the original size.

questions.question: After resizing, the ratio of width and height can change.
questions.explanation: The lecture explicitly warns that ratio can change after resizing.

questions.question: What type of argument does resize() expect for the new size?
questions.explanation: The lecture says the argument of resize() is a tuple.

questions.question: Which resampling filter is shown in the lecture example?
questions.explanation: The resize example uses resample=Image.Resampling.LANCZOS.

questions.question: Which method creates a copy of an image object?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses img.copy().

questions.question: Which method pastes one image into another?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses paste(image, location).

questions.question: What is location = (20,30) in img.paste(copied_img, location)?

options.A: The top-left target position where the copied image is pasted
options.B: The width and height of the pasted image

questions.explanation: The location tuple gives the paste position.

questions.question: Which method is shown only for debugging or previewing the image?
questions.explanation: The examples use img.show() for debugging purposes.

questions.question: Which import is needed to draw shapes on images?
questions.explanation: The lecture imports Image and ImageDraw from PIL.

questions.question: Which object is created before drawing shapes on an image?
questions.explanation: The drawing object is created with ImageDraw.Draw(img).

questions.question: Drawing with ImageDraw modifies the original image object.
questions.explanation: The lecture states that the original image is actually modified.

questions.question: Which method draws a single point?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses pic.point(...).

questions.question: Which method draws a line?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses pic.line([...], width=..., fill=...).

questions.question: Which method draws a rectangle?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses pic.rectangle(...).

questions.question: Which method draws an ellipse?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses pic.ellipse(...).

questions.question: Which method adds text to an image with ImageDraw?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses ImageDraw.text.

questions.question: Which module is imported to use a TrueType font in Pillow?
questions.explanation: The lecture imports ImageFont from PIL.

questions.question: Which call loads a TrueType font from a font path and size?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses ImageFont.truetype(path, size).

questions.question: ImageDraw can use built-in fonts or OpenType/TrueType fonts available in the system.
questions.explanation: The lecture says built-in and OT/TT fonts can be used.

questions.question: Which module is installed for creating plots?
questions.explanation: The lecture installs matplotlib for plots.

questions.question: Which import alias is used in the lecture for pyplot?

options.C: from matplotlib import plt as pyplot

questions.explanation: The lecture imports pyplot as plt.

questions.question: Which function sets the X axis label?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses plt.xlabel(...).

questions.question: Which function sets the Y axis label?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses plt.ylabel(...).

questions.question: Which function sets the chart title?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses plt.title(...).

questions.question: Which function displays the plot window?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses plt.show().

questions.question: If you call plt.plot([1,2,4,8]) without X values, what X values does matplotlib assume?

options.D: No X values; it raises TypeError

questions.explanation: When X values are not provided, matplotlib uses indexes 0, 1, 2, and so on.

questions.question: By default, plt.plot draws lines.
questions.explanation: The lecture states that by default a plot is drawn with lines.

questions.question: In the format string "r^", what does r mean?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists r as red.

questions.question: In the format string "bo", what does b mean?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists b as blue.

questions.question: In the format string "r^", what does ^ mean?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists ^ as triangles.

questions.question: In the format string "bo", what does o mean?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists o as circles.

questions.question: Which marker character is listed for squares?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists s as squares.

questions.question: Which function sets the viewport of the plot?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses axis() to set the viewport.

questions.question: What is the order of values in plt.axis([-1, 5, 0, 4]) according to the lecture comment?
questions.explanation: The comment says order: xmin, xmax, ...; full order is xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax.

questions.question: Which function saves a plot to a file?
questions.explanation: The lecture uses savefig to save a plot.

questions.question: Which method creates line plots?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists plot as line plots.

questions.question: Which method creates a series of points?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists scatter as a series of points.

questions.question: Which method creates a bar chart?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists bar as bar chart.

questions.question: scatter is listed as the function for bar charts.
questions.explanation: scatter is for a series of points; bar is for bar charts.

questions.question: bar is listed as the function for a bar chart.
questions.explanation: The lecture lists bar as bar chart.

questions.question: plot is listed as the function for line plots.
questions.explanation: The lecture lists plot as line plots.

questions.question: savefig() is used to display a chart interactively and cannot save files.
questions.explanation: savefig saves the plot to a file.

questions.question: plt.show() is used in the first plot example after plt.plot(...).
questions.explanation: The first plot example ends with plt.show().

questions.question: In the lecture example plt.plot([1,2,3,4],[1,4,8,16],"r^", [1,2,3,4],[-1,0,1,2],"bo"), how many data series are plotted?
questions.explanation: The call provides two X/Y/format groups: one red triangle series and one blue circle series.

questions.question: Which format string in the lecture example draws blue circles?
questions.explanation: b means blue and o means circle.

questions.question: Which format string in the lecture example draws red triangles?
questions.explanation: r means red and ^ means triangle.

questions.question: python-docx is the package used for image cropping and rotation.
questions.explanation: Image cropping and rotation are done with Pillow/PIL, not python-docx.

questions.question: Pillow is used for manipulating pictures, while python-docx is used for DOCX documents.
questions.explanation: This matches the separation in the lecture.

questions.question: matplotlib is the package used in the lecture for creating plots and charts.
questions.explanation: The lecture installs matplotlib for plots.

questions.question: python-docx is installed with pip install matplotlib.
questions.explanation: python-docx is installed with pip install python-docx.

questions.question: ImageDraw.Draw(img) is a Pillow concept, not a matplotlib concept.
questions.explanation: ImageDraw is imported from PIL.

questions.question: plt.plot([1,2,4,8]) creates a Pillow image object.
questions.explanation: plt.plot creates a matplotlib plot, not a Pillow image object.

questions.question: A style can include settings such as font face, italic, and bold.
questions.explanation: The lecture describes style as settings such as font face, italic, bold.

questions.question: A paragraph can consist of multiple runs when different parts of the paragraph use different styles.
questions.explanation: Runs represent sequences of characters using one style.

questions.question: Image.open("example.gif").save("example.png") can convert a GIF file to PNG.
questions.explanation: The lecture example opens a GIF and saves it as PNG; PIL recognizes extensions.

questions.question: Image.new("RGBA", (200,100)) creates a 200 by 100 image.
questions.explanation: The size tuple is width 200 and height 100.

questions.question: new2_img = my_img.resize((w+100, h)) changes width but keeps the old height.
questions.explanation: The tuple passes a wider width and the same height h.

questions.question: img.crop((100,100,400,200)) returns a new cropped image assigned in the example to img2.
questions.explanation: The example assigns the crop result to img2.

questions.question: img.show() is used in the lecture examples for debugging or preview purposes.
questions.explanation: The lecture comment says it is for debugging purposes.

questions.question: plt.axis([-1,5,0,4]) sets only the X-axis limits.
questions.explanation: The four values set both X and Y limits.
